dependencies.classification_rationale: The roadmap explicitly schedules environment, tenant, and authority-profile enumeration before third-party provisioning begins.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Low sensitivity configuration, but foundational for later secret scopes and authority separation.
notes: This is a control-plane dependency, not an external SaaS by itself.
Later authority, IdP, email, and edge rows depend on this matrix for stable separation.
dependencies.purpose: Freeze the canonical environment, tenant, authority-profile, callback-host, and partition matrix that all later registrations consume.
required_features: stable environment names and promotion lanes
sandbox versus production authority profile separation

source_refs.rationale: The provisioning wave starts by enumerating environments, tenants, and authority profiles.
Authority objects freeze provider environment, scope, and binding identity, so those profiles must exist before registration.
Authority links and delegation edges need a canonical environment and authority partition basis.

dependencies.classification_rationale: The roadmap directly calls for a browser automation workspace and explicit anti-bot/manual checkpoint evidence capture.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Privileged provisioning sessions, screenshots, and provider-console metadata.
notes: This row is required even though it is not a production runtime service.
Manual checkpoint capture is a first-class part of the provisioning contract, not an exception path.
dependencies.purpose: Provide a headed browser automation harness for third-party provisioning, evidence capture, and repeatable console operations.
required_features: persistent browser profiles for controlled admin sessions
secure secret injection from vault-managed inputs
headed execution for MFA and CAPTCHA checkpoints
stable callback host testing across local, CI, review, and staging

source_refs.rationale: Provisioning work requires a reusable browser automation workspace.
The roadmap expects checkpoint evidence capture when portals block full automation.

dependencies.classification_rationale: The roadmap explicitly schedules creation of an HMRC developer-hub account and project workspace before app registration.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Privileged provider-console access and regulated app-management metadata.
notes: Human-operated provider identities are expected; the system should not persist personal console credentials as product secrets.
dependencies.purpose: Establish the authority provider account and project workspace used to manage application registrations and provider-side settings.
required_features: provider developer account or project workspace

source_refs.rationale: The roadmap requires a developer-hub workspace before sandbox app registration can begin.
Authority integration is an external provider boundary rather than an internal-only API.

dependencies.classification_rationale: The roadmap explicitly calls for a distinct sandbox app registration for obligations, calculations, and submissions.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Authority client identifiers, client secrets, and sandbox scope bindings.
notes: Sandbox app credentials must remain separate from production credentials and rotation timelines.
dependencies.purpose: Register the sandbox authority client used for non-production obligations, calculation, and submission flows.
required_features: sandbox client identifier and secret issuance
export-ready credential metadata for vault ingestion

source_refs.rationale: The roadmap creates a dedicated sandbox application for obligations, calculations, and submissions.
Authority traffic is bound to a specific client and token profile, so sandbox registration is a separate dependency.
Imported authorization and authority-link rules assume stable client identities per provider environment.

dependencies.classification_rationale: The corpus freezes provider_environment in authority bindings, so production registration cannot be collapsed into the sandbox record even though the checklist names the sandbox task first.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Live authority client secrets and production integration approval metadata.
notes: This is explicit in the dependency model even though the early provisioning checklist does not yet split out a dedicated production registration card.
dependencies.purpose: Register the production authority client and approval boundary used for live obligations, filings, and reconciliations.
required_features: production client identifier and secret issuance
live redirect URI and callback host approval
scope approval and go-live review evidence
production rate-limit and support contact metadata

source_refs.rationale: Environment and authority-profile planning implies a live provider environment alongside sandbox.
Vault export and rotation boundaries imply separate live credentials as well as sandbox credentials.
Authority bindings freeze provider environment and API version, requiring a distinct production registration.

dependencies.classification_rationale: The roadmap separately schedules redirect URI, scope, and callback endpoint configuration after app registration.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Callback endpoint metadata, approved scopes, and provider-console settings.
notes: Ephemeral review environments are a poor fit for provider callback registration because hostnames are unstable.
dependencies.purpose: Bind authority apps to the exact redirect URIs, callback endpoints, and granted scopes required by the controlled authority gateway.

source_refs.rationale: Redirect URIs, scopes, and callback endpoints are an explicit provisioning step.
Preflight checks require exact endpoint and scope readiness before live authority operations proceed.
Inbound callbacks are part of the normalized authority ingress boundary and must be registered precisely.

dependencies.classification_rationale: The roadmap explicitly schedules validation of fraud-prevention header setup and sandbox profile bindings as a separate dependency boundary.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Regulated device and operator posture metadata plus provider binding evidence.
notes: Fraud-prevention profile readiness is not equivalent to client registration; it must remain separately testable.
dependencies.purpose: Bind provider-required fraud-prevention and environment-profile metadata to each authority client and execution environment.
required_features: per-environment device and operator posture separation

source_refs.rationale: The roadmap calls for explicit fraud-prevention and sandbox-profile validation.
Fraud-prevention posture is mandatory runtime law and therefore a distinct setup dependency.

dependencies.classification_rationale: Authority transport, callbacks, idempotency, reconciliation, and out-of-band handling are explicit algorithmic boundaries.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Authority tokens, client secrets, taxpayer identifiers, and provider-side response evidence.
notes: The register treats the runtime authority edge as mandatory even though specific vendors or jurisdictions may vary.
dependencies.purpose: Support live authority calculation, submission, duplicate handling, reconciliation, and callback ingress against the external authority of record.

source_refs.rationale: The contract defines the authority boundary as a controlled external interaction surface.
Runtime interaction requires exact idempotency and hash handling against the provider API.
Authority truth and internal truth must reconcile through a real provider interface, not an internal-only API.

dependencies.classification_rationale: Federated product identity through OIDC/OAuth 2.0 or equivalent is an explicit runtime-security requirement.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Operator and customer authentication events, client secrets, and session trust state.
notes: The final session model is internal, but the federation surface itself remains an external dependency.
dependencies.purpose: Provide external identity federation, short-lived sessions, and step-up-capable authentication for browser and native surfaces.
required_features: OIDC or equivalent short-lived identity flows
federated sign-in for browser and native clients

source_refs.rationale: The security contract explicitly requires federated product identity through OIDC/OAuth 2.0 or equivalent.
Machine actors and federated authorization rules require an external identity boundary.

dependencies.classification_rationale: The roadmap explicitly schedules creation of the IdP tenant and application clients as a distinct provisioning step.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Client secrets, application metadata, and tenant administration state.
notes: This row captures concrete tenant and client objects, not the abstract federation capability.
dependencies.purpose: Provision concrete IdP tenants and client registrations for web, native, and service-principal surfaces.
required_features: separate client applications by surface or environment
redirect URI and logout URI registration

source_refs.rationale: The roadmap creates IdP tenants and application clients as a first-class provisioning dependency.
The federation requirement becomes concrete through per-surface client registrations.

dependencies.classification_rationale: The roadmap separately schedules roles, scopes, MFA, step-up, and session-policy configuration inside the chosen IdP.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Authorization policy and session-governance metadata.
notes: This row is configuration, not a new vendor choice.
dependencies.purpose: Bind product authorization semantics to the concrete IdP configuration for scopes, MFA, step-up, session lifetime, and role posture.

source_refs.rationale: The roadmap treats MFA, step-up, session, and role-policy setup as its own dependency boundary.
Step-up and non-delegable action rules must be reflected in concrete IdP policy configuration.
Session and step-up trust rules require explicit policy enforcement, not just raw client registration.

dependencies.classification_rationale: The roadmap explicitly calls for a delivery-provider account and sender-domain setup, while the collaboration contract only makes email optional.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Recipient addresses, message metadata, and email API credentials.
notes: Email is optional at the algorithm layer, but once selected it requires real provisioning and domain-proof workflows.
dependencies.purpose: Provision the optional external email channel used for customer notifications and support communication where the product elects to send email.
required_features: outbound API or SMTP credential issuance

source_refs.rationale: The roadmap provisions a delivery-provider account and sender domain explicitly.
Customer notifications are in-app and optionally email, so email remains a separate, non-core channel dependency.

dependencies.classification_rationale: The roadmap separately schedules template, webhook, and delivery-callback configuration after the account exists.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Email event metadata, webhook secrets, and customer-visible message templates.
notes: Delivery callbacks must not leak internal-only state into customer-visible workflows.
dependencies.purpose: Configure message templates, delivery event webhooks, and callback authentication for the selected email provider.

source_refs.rationale: The roadmap isolates email templates, webhooks, and callbacks as a dedicated setup step.
The notification contract requires delivery events to stay deduplicated and visibility-safe, which provider callbacks must support.

dependencies.classification_rationale: The roadmap explicitly names push or device messaging, but the algorithm corpus treats external push as an embodiment-dependent extension rather than a universal requirement.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Device tokens, notification payload metadata, and messaging service keys.
notes: Treat this as embodiment-conditional, not a universal algorithm requirement.
dependencies.purpose: Provision external device-messaging credentials if the chosen native or multi-device embodiment needs remote push delivery.

source_refs.rationale: The roadmap includes a push or device-messaging project and keys.
The native embodiment expects notification surfaces, which can drive a later decision to use an external messaging project.
source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: 11. Security and runtime posture for the desktop client

dependencies.classification_rationale: The algorithm explicitly requires OpenTelemetry-compatible telemetry, but the checklist's separate error-monitoring workspace is an optional overlay rather than the core observability truth store.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Stack traces, crash diagnostics, release metadata, and ingest tokens.
notes: Do not mistake this for the authoritative audit or OpenTelemetry backend.
dependencies.purpose: Provide a convenience layer for grouped error triage, release markers, and client-surface crash capture beyond the mandatory telemetry baseline.
required_features: link-back to manifest and trace identifiers

source_refs.rationale: The roadmap provides a dedicated error-monitoring workspace and project tokens.
Operational telemetry is mandatory, but a branded error-monitoring workspace is not the normative audit store.

dependencies.classification_rationale: The roadmap marks the helpdesk workspace as conditional ('if selected'), and the algorithm models help as a product capability rather than a required external vendor.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Support transcripts, portal help context, and integration API tokens.
notes: PortalHelpRequest remains an internal artifact even when mirrored into an external system.
dependencies.purpose: Optionally sync contextual help or operator-assist workflows into an external helpdesk or CRM system.

source_refs.rationale: The roadmap treats helpdesk integration as conditional on selection.
The product has a first-class help route, but the contract does not require an external helpdesk vendor.

dependencies.classification_rationale: The corpus keeps generic OCR outside the core invention boundary and the roadmap explicitly allows either a managed OCR project or a self-hosted decision.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Uploaded documents, extracted tax fields, and extraction service credentials if managed.
notes: Do not overstate OCR as mandatory for algorithm conformance; it is a pluggable implementation choice.
dependencies.purpose: Optionally provide document text extraction when the chosen implementation uses OCR to assist intake or evidence review.
required_features: document extraction API or self-hosted runtime
object-store integration for source artifact retention

source_refs.rationale: The roadmap makes OCR explicitly pluggable: managed project or self-host decision.
OCR is treated as an optional externalized connector boundary that still needs runtime hardening when present.
Generic OCR remains adjacent infrastructure rather than core algorithm law.

dependencies.classification_rationale: The upload and collaboration contracts make scan/quarantine gating mandatory even though the final vendor or self-host strategy is left open.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Raw uploaded binaries, quarantine evidence, and scanner service identities.
notes: Capability is mandatory; vendor selection remains open.
dependencies.purpose: Scan uploaded files before download or customer-visible publication, quarantine unsafe content, and preserve typed scan-state evidence.
required_features: pending, clean, and quarantined scan states
typed reason codes surfaced to product read models

source_refs.rationale: The roadmap requires either a scanning/quarantine service or a recorded self-host choice.
The portal contract requires explicit transfer, scan, validation, acceptance, rejection, and quarantine states.
Staged uploads remain non-downloadable until malware scanning and visibility validation succeed.

dependencies.classification_rationale: The runtime topology and hardening contract explicitly require a governed token vault or secret store for raw authority credentials.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Highest sensitivity secret and token material.
notes: This is the inventory anchor for all later external credentials.
dependencies.purpose: Hold raw authority tokens, app secrets, and integration credentials behind audited policy and rotation boundaries.
required_features: auditability of secret access and rotation

source_refs.rationale: The runtime topology names token vault and KMS/HSM as first-class components.
Raw authority tokens must live only in a governed token vault or secret store.

dependencies.classification_rationale: The hardening contract explicitly roots envelope keys and protected secrets in KMS/HSM-class controls.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Root cryptographic material and high-impact admin roles.
notes: Secrets manager, build signing, and storage encryption all fan out from this boundary.
dependencies.purpose: Provide the root cryptographic trust boundary for envelope encryption, secret protection, and signing-key custody.
required_features: per-tenant or per-sensitivity envelope key support

source_refs.rationale: The topology explicitly names KMS/HSM alongside the token vault boundary.
Envelope keys and secret protection are rooted in KMS/HSM master-key material.

dependencies.classification_rationale: The deployment topology explicitly requires a primary control store as the durable system of record.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Regulated control-plane truth and application service credentials.
notes: The checklist later names PostgreSQL explicitly, but the algorithmic need is for a transactional control store first.
dependencies.purpose: Persist manifests, workflow truth, authority state, and control-plane records with transactional guarantees.

source_refs.rationale: The runtime topology names a primary control store as the durable truth boundary.
Migration rules assume a transactional primary store with governed schema progression.

dependencies.classification_rationale: The deployment topology and observability contract explicitly separate append-only audit evidence from mutable operational telemetry.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Compliance-significant audit evidence and write credentials.
notes: Do not collapse this into the generic OpenTelemetry backend.
dependencies.purpose: Persist immutable compliance-significant events and audit evidence outside disposable telemetry streams.
required_features: correlation to manifest and authority identifiers
separate access control from generic observability signals

source_refs.rationale: The topology names an append-only audit store as a distinct component.
Audit history must remain distinct from sampled telemetry and therefore needs its own governed store.

dependencies.classification_rationale: The runtime topology explicitly requires an object store, and the upload contracts require quarantine-aware artifact segregation.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Uploaded documents, exports, quarantined binaries, and storage access roles.
notes: Quarantine storage is part of the same capability boundary but must stay logically distinct.
dependencies.purpose: Store evidence artifacts, uploads, exports, quarantined files, and other large binary or retained objects.
required_features: bucket or container segmentation for evidence, exports, and quarantine
signed URL or preview delivery controls
event hooks for scan and publish workflows

source_refs.rationale: The topology explicitly names an object store for retained artifacts and evidence.
Upload staging and quarantine require explicit storage segregation before publication.

dependencies.classification_rationale: The runtime topology explicitly requires a queue/broker for outbox, inbox, and worker coordination, even though it may never be the system of record.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Opaque refs, timing metadata, and broker access credentials.
notes: Queue loss must remain recoverable from durable truth.
dependencies.purpose: Coordinate worker execution, inbox/outbox delivery, and asynchronous integration flows without becoming durable legal truth.

source_refs.rationale: The topology explicitly names a queue or broker for delivery fabric and worker coordination.
Inbound callbacks and worker results enter through transactional inbox and dedupe layers before mutation.

dependencies.classification_rationale: Disposable caches and resume stores are explicit runtime components, with strict cache-isolation and rebuild rules.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Masked projections, resume tokens, and cache access credentials.
notes: This is a real externalized runtime capability even though its stored contents remain disposable.
dependencies.purpose: Hold resumable stream cursors, disposable caches, and other rebuildable acceleration state without widening security or truth boundaries.
required_features: explicit purge or reject on security drift

source_refs.rationale: The topology includes a read-side projector or stream broker plus disposable caches and rebuildable read state.
Cache and resume state are governed security boundaries rather than generic performance-only features.
source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: 6. Data protection, privacy, and cache safety

dependencies.classification_rationale: The observability contract explicitly grounds telemetry in OpenTelemetry-style traces, metrics, and logs while separating them from audit evidence.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Operational, security, and privacy telemetry plus ingest credentials.
notes: The capability is mandatory even when the backend is self-hosted rather than vendor SaaS.
dependencies.purpose: Collect and persist traces, metrics, and logs with mandatory correlation keys and controlled access boundaries.

source_refs.rationale: The signal model explicitly separates audit evidence from operational, security, and privacy telemetry.
Metric, trace, and log families are mandatory runtime capabilities even though product choice is open.

dependencies.classification_rationale: Build signing, SBOMs, provenance attestations, and release admission checks are explicit hardening requirements.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Build artifacts, registry credentials, provenance metadata, and signing-key references.
notes: The capability is explicit even if the eventual ADR selects a specific registry or attestation stack later.
dependencies.purpose: Store deployable artifacts and provide vulnerability scanning, signing, provenance attestation, and release-admission evidence.

source_refs.rationale: Supply-chain integrity explicitly requires SBOMs, vulnerability scanning, signed build outputs, and provenance checks.
The promotion pipeline builds, signs, and verifies release artifacts before promotion.

dependencies.classification_rationale: TLS for external traffic is explicit, while the checklist packages DNS, WAF, and edge delivery into the practical provisioning surface.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Domain-control credentials, certificate private keys, and ingress policy configuration.
notes: Ephemeral review hosts should not be used as the canonical provider callback target even if they exist at the edge.
dependencies.purpose: Provide public DNS, TLS termination or certificate automation, callback host stability, and protective edge controls for exposed endpoints.
required_features: stable authority and IdP callback hostnames

source_refs.rationale: The roadmap packages DNS, TLS, WAF, and edge delivery as a single provisioning step.
TLS for all external traffic is mandatory, and stable ingress is a prerequisite for provider callbacks and identity flows.
source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: 4. Browser, native-client, API, and transport hardening

dependencies.classification_rationale: The roadmap explicitly schedules CI/CD runners, environment secrets, and ephemeral preview accounts as a combined control-plane dependency.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Deploy credentials, runner registration tokens, preview-account secrets, and build logs.
notes: Preview environments are useful for internal smoke tests, but they remain a poor source of authority callback hostnames.
dependencies.purpose: Provide isolated automation runners, gated secret injection, and preview environments for repeatable delivery and provisioning verification.

source_refs.rationale: The roadmap explicitly provisions CI/CD runners, environment secrets, and preview accounts.
Promotion requires repeated gated environments and reliable automation execution.
Release admission depends on automated policy checks and secret-hygiene enforcement.

dependencies.classification_rationale: The native blueprint, deployment contract, and security gates all explicitly require code signing, notarization, hardened runtime, and Keychain-safe storage for shipped macOS builds.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Apple signing certificates, notary API keys, and desktop credential-handling trust policy.
notes: Treat Apple-side trust as a distinct external dependency rather than a generic CI detail.
dependencies.purpose: Provide the Apple-side signing, notarization, and local-trust chain needed to ship the native macOS operator workspace safely.
required_features: Keychain-safe handling for native session material
dependencies.source_heading_or_logical_block: 11. Security and runtime posture for the desktop client

source_refs.rationale: The native blueprint requires code signing, notarization, hardened runtime, and Keychain-only credential posture.
source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: 11. Security and runtime posture for the desktop client

source_refs.rationale: No production desktop client may ship without signature, notarization, and hardened-runtime policy.
BuildArtifact promotion explicitly includes signing and notarization when the desktop app ships.

dependencies.classification_rationale: The deployment topology explicitly names a desktop release/update channel with staged distribution and emergency revocation.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Signed release artifacts, update metadata, and channel-publishing credentials.
notes: This is separate from signing and notarization; it governs ongoing distribution and revocation.
dependencies.purpose: Distribute signed desktop builds, segment update channels, and support revocation or kill-switch behavior for the macOS operator workspace.

source_refs.rationale: The deployment topology explicitly names the desktop release/update channel as a runtime component.
Delivery sequencing assumes a real signed-distribution mechanism rather than ad hoc local builds.

dependencies.classification_rationale: The northbound command and session surfaces are explicitly product-internal APIs, not third-party dependencies.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Product session state and internal authorization metadata.
notes: This row exists to prevent later provisioning work from misclassifying product APIs as external services.
dependencies.purpose: Expose internal command ingress, session continuity, anti-CSRF posture, and browser/native state coordination for product clients.

source_refs.rationale: The contract defines these APIs as internal system surfaces even though they are exposed over HTTP.
Session and browser-state rules are part of the internal product contract, not an external service dependency.

dependencies.classification_rationale: Read models, stream endpoints, and upload-session APIs are internal northbound product surfaces even when they touch externalized storage or scanning infrastructure underneath.
dependencies.data_or_credential_sensitivity: Customer-safe projections, upload-session state, and stream resume metadata.
notes: This row separates the external infrastructure from the internal product contract that consumes it.
dependencies.purpose: Expose governed internal read surfaces, stream/reconnect flows, and upload-session APIs to browser and native clients.
required_features: typed scan and limitation posture projection

source_refs.rationale: Admin, portal, and collaboration reads remain internal product APIs rather than third-party services.
Upload staging uses internal session APIs layered over storage and scanning dependencies.
Stream and resume semantics belong to the product API surface, not the external dependency list.
